title: Docker Compose Skeleton
description: docker-compose.yml with three services (api, mcp, ollama) using GPU profiles (cpu/gpu-amd/gpu-nvidia) via YAML anchors. Includes ollama-pull-llama init containers, network alias for ollama, and service_completed_successfully dependency. .env.example with COMPOSE_PROFILES, OLLAMA_MODEL, OLLAMA_BASE_URL, MCP_BASE_URL, IBGE_BASE_URL.
steps: Define x-ollama anchor: image ollama/ollama:latest, container_name ollama, port 11434, volume ollama_data, network alias ollama.
Define x-init-ollama anchor: runs ollama pull $OLLAMA_MODEL after a 3s sleep, OLLAMA_HOST=ollama:11434.
Create profile services: ollama-cpu, ollama-gpu-amd (rocm image + /dev/kfd /dev/dri), ollama-gpu-nvidia (nvidia deploy resources).
Create matching ollama-pull-llama-{cpu,gpu-amd,gpu-nvidia} init services, each depends_on its ollama variant.
api service: depends_on all three pull containers with condition service_completed_successfully, required false; restart on-failure.
mcp service: healthcheck curl -sf http://localhost:8001/health.
Create .env.example and .gitignore (.env, __pycache__, .venv).

title: MCP Service Dockerfile and Requirements
description: Dockerfile for the mcp_server service. Runs FastMCP on port 8001 via streamable-http transport.
steps: mcp_server/Dockerfile: python:3.14-slim base, WORKDIR /app, copy requirements.txt, pip install, copy mcp_server/, expose 8001, CMD uvicorn.
Create mcp_server/__init__.py and mcp_server/tools/__init__.py.

title: API Service Dockerfile and Requirements
description: Root Dockerfile for the api service. Minimal FastAPI app with lifespan.
steps: Root Dockerfile: python:3.14-slim base, WORKDIR /app, copy requirements.txt, pip install, copy src/, expose 8000, CMD uvicorn src.main:app.
requirements.txt and pyproject.toml with all pinned dependencies including ruff under [dependency-groups] dev.
src/main.py: FastAPI app with asynccontextmanager lifespan, includes chat and health routers.
src/env.py: reads OLLAMA_MODEL, OLLAMA_BASE_URL, MCP_BASE_URL, IBGE_BASE_URL from environment.

title: MCP Tool: ibge_get_states
description: get_states tool returning all Brazilian states from IBGE /v1/localidades/estados. Registered in FastMCP server.
steps: mcp_server/ibge_client.py: IBGEClient with httpx.AsyncClient, async get(path, params) method.
mcp_server/tools/location.py: async def get_states(ibge_client) calling /v1/localidades/estados.
mcp_server/server.py: FastMCP app with streamable-http transport on port 8001, registers ibge_get_states tool.

title: MCP Tool: ibge_get_municipalities
description: get_municipalities tool returning all municipalities for a given UF code from IBGE.
steps: mcp_server/tools/location.py: async def get_municipalities(uf, ibge_client) uppercasing uf and calling /v1/localidades/estados/{uf}/municipios.
Register ibge_get_municipalities in mcp_server/server.py.

title: LangChain Agent
description: LangGraph agent built with create_agent(), using ChatOllama and MCP tools. No custom state or tool tracking.
steps: src/agent/prompt.py: SYSTEM_PROMPT instructing the agent to answer questions about Brazil using IBGE tools, respond in the same language as the question, never fabricate statistics.

src/agent/agent.py: async def agent_call(question) — instantiates ChatOllama(model, base_url), calls load_mcp_tools(), builds agent via create_agent(llm, tools), invokes with system + user messages, returns last message content.

title: MCP Client Bootstrap
description: src/mcp/client.py using langchain-mcp-adapters MultiServerMCPClient to load tools from the MCP server.
steps: async def load_mcp_tools() -> list[BaseTool]: creates MultiServerMCPClient with MCP_BASE_URL and streamable-http transport, calls get_tools(), returns tool list.

title: POST /chat Endpoint
description: POST /chat accepts a question string and returns the agent's answer.
steps: src/routes/responses.py: ChatRequest(question: str), ChatResponse(answer: str), HealthResponse(healthy: bool).
src/routes/chat.py: async def chat(body: ChatRequest) calls agent_call(body.question), returns ChatResponse.

title: GET /health Endpoint
description: GET /health returns {healthy: true}. Used by Docker Compose healthcheck.
steps: src/routes/health.py: returns HealthResponse(healthy=True).

title: GitHub Actions CI Pipeline
description: Single lint job on push/PR to main: installs requirements.txt and runs ruff check.
steps: .github/workflows/ci.yml: trigger on push+PR to main, job ruff on ubuntu-latest, setup-python 3.11, pip install -r requirements.txt, run ruff check .
dependabot.yml: weekly updates for pip, actions, and docker.

title: MLflow Monitoring
description: Add MLflow tracking to the agent to log inputs, outputs, tool calls, latency, and model metadata for each invocation.
steps: Add mlflow to requirements.txt and pyproject.toml dependencies.
Add MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI env var to .env.example and docker-compose.yml (default http://mlflow:5000).
Optionally add an mlflow service to docker-compose.yml.
In src/agent/agent.py, wrap agent_call with an mlflow run: log question, model name, latency_ms, tools_used, and answer as params/metrics/artifacts.
Create a dedicated src/observability/mlflow.py helper to encapsulate run creation and logging.

title: Error Handling (try/except blocks)
description: Add structured error handling across routes and agent calls so failures return meaningful HTTP responses instead of 500 Internal Server Error.
steps: In src/routes/chat.py, wrap agent_call in try/except and return HTTP 502 on LLM/MCP connection errors, HTTP 422 on validation errors.
In src/agent/agent.py, catch httpx.ConnectError and raise a custom AgentUnavailableError with a clear message.
In src/mcp/client.py, catch connection errors on load_mcp_tools and raise RuntimeError with context.
Add a global FastAPI exception handler in src/main.py for unhandled exceptions returning {"detail": "internal error"} with HTTP 500.
Ensure all error paths are logged with the Python logging module.
